title: Solve problem to Install Nodejs20 on Ubuntu 22.04
subtitle: How install nodejs 20 on ubuntu

content.text: Solve problem to Install Nodejs20 on Ubuntu 22.04

content.alt: Solve problem to Install Nodejs20 on Ubuntu 22.04

content.code: SCRIPT DEPRECATION WARNINGThis script, located at https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_X, used to install Node.js is deprecated now and will eventually be made inactive.Please visit the NodeSource distributions Github and follow the instructions to migrate your repo. https://github.com/nodesource/distributionsThe NodeSource Node.js Linux distributions GitHub repository contains information about which versions of Node.js and which Linux distributions are supported and how to install it. https://github.com/nodesource/distributionsSCRIPT DEPRECATION WARNING

## You may also need development tools to build native addons:     sudo apt-get install gcc g++ make## To install the Yarn package manager, run:     curl -sL https://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian/pubkey.gpg | gpg --dearmor | sudo tee /usr/share/keyrings/yarnkey.gpg >/dev/null     echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/yarnkey.gpg] https://dl.yarnpkg.com/debian stable main" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/yarn.list     sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install yarn
sudo apt-get remove nodejssudo apt-get remove npmsudo apt-get purge nodejssudo apt-get updatewhich node

content.html: If you have root access, you can omit the ‘sudo’ command as you already have full administrative privileges.

content.text: 1. Download and import the Nodesource GPG key

content.code: sudo apt-get updatesudo apt-get install -y ca-certificates curl gnupgsudo mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyringscurl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/gpgkey/nodesource-repo.gpg.key | sudo gpg --dearmor -o /etc/apt/keyrings/nodesource.gpg

content.html: Optional: NODE_MAJOR can be changed depending on the version you need.

content.code: sudo apt-get updatesudo apt-get install nodejs -y

content.text: dpkg: error processing archive /var/cache/apt/archives/nodejs_20.5.1-deb-1nodesource1_amd64.deb ( — unpack): trying to overwrite ‘/usr/include/node/common.gypi’, which is also in package libnode-dev 12.22.9~dfsg-1ubuntu3.2dpkg-deb: error: paste subprocess was killed by signal (Broken pipe)

content.code: sudo apt-get --fix-broken installsudo apt autoremovesudo apt-get install -y nodejs
